title: SDL Dependency
description: A Dependency is an external resource that this service calls or reads from — another service, a database, a cache, an event broker, or a third-party API. Dependencies are DL inter-Container Connect candidates: by reading all services' declared dependencies, a synthesis tool constructs PlatformConnect objects at layer_platform. Each Dependency declares which client class wraps it, what calls are made, and how failures are handled.

dl.notes: Dependency = candidate inter-Container Connect. The actual Connect (PlatformConnect) is materialized by a synthesis tool at layer_platform. The service (child Container) declares its outbound dependencies; the platform (parent Container) owns the resolved Connect.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this dependency within the service.

kind.description: What category of external resource this is. See stdlib/dependency-kinds.json for ai_hint per kind.

description.description: What this dependency provides and why this service uses it.

interface.description: How this service calls the dependency — protocol and specific operations invoked.

calls.description: The specific operations or endpoints this service invokes on the dependency.

reliability.description: How this service handles failures from this dependency.

client_ref.description: The client class or module in the codebase that wraps this dependency.
